title: Configure WordPress Website Using AWS Console Challenge

tasks.title: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.
On the AWS sign-in page, Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the challenge.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.

In this challenge, your open-source content management system (CMS) technical skills are put to the test. You'll be given a requirement and you have to reach it using your knowledge of Web Server softwares, php, apache server and MySQL databases that are used for this challenge. This Challenge will help you understand the real-time scenarios.
Sign in to AWS Management Console

A company XYZ wants to build their own WordPress Website in AWS Console with EC2 Instance Contents using MariaDB Database, Apache HTTP Server, PHP and WordPress Scripts. The below are the tasks that need to be performed by you in order to create the stack.
Create EC2 Instance with AMI type as Amazon Linux 2023 and instance type as t2.micro with SSH, HTTP, HTTPS.
Configure Apache Server and MariaDB
Add group write permissions, set ID and change the directory and its subdirectories
Configure MariaDB Server
Install the Administration of MySQL over Web with phpMyAdmin and create folder.
Install WordPress:
Log into the database server to create a user and password for the MySQL database in the root user.
Create a Database in phpMyAdmin.
Grant full privileges for the database to the WordPress.
Flush the database privileges.
Create and Edit WordPress Config File:
Transfer the contents of the wp-config-sample.php file to a new file named wp-config.php.
Look for the section under Authentication Unique Keys and Salts.
Visit https://api.wordpress.org/secret-key/1.1/salt/ to obtain a random set of key values.
Create a folder mywordpresswebsite.
Provide Permissions for WordPress to use Permalinks:
Allow all overrides in the Apache document root.
Provide file permissions for the Apache web server.
Recursively modify the /var/www file permissions and restart the Apache web server.
Run the WordPress Installation Script:
Make sure that the database and httpd server launch on system setup.
Test for confirmation if the Apache web server (httpd) and database server are running.
In a web browser, type the below URL to see the php admin page:
http://YourIPAddress/mywordpresswebssite
Enter Username as John Doe and the login password
Click on the Validate to complete the challenge.
After Validation Terminate all the resources.
Sign out of the AWS Account.
You have Successfully completed the challenge.
Click on End Challenge button from the lab console and wait till the process gets complete
